summary: Completed testing of RX Expresss pharmacy delivery service features. All 8 requested features verified working: Admin Orders refresh button, Refrigerated orders display with blue/frost styling and ❄️ badge, Admin can mark orders as refrigerated via edit modal checkbox, Driver deliveries API excludes in_transit orders, In Transit scan unassigns driver for admin reassignment, Delivering Now status shows POD buttons directly, Driver delivery history endpoint works, API includes isRefrigerated field.

minor.issue: Was missing isRefrigerated field in response - FIXED during testing

critical_code_review_comments: The /api/orders/{id} endpoint was missing isRefrigerated field - fixed by adding to Select projection
seed_data_creation: Order ID 1 (RX-QNS00001) is marked as refrigerated for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features working correctly. Order 1 is refrigerated and visible with blue styling on Admin Orders page. Driver portal shows POD buttons directly for 'delivering_now' status without requiring scan. In-transit workflow correctly unassigns driver. Print functionality opens print dialog (not save dialog) using window.print().

evidence: Button with data-testid='refresh-orders-btn' present in top bar
Clicking refresh button reloads order data
Button displays refresh icon and 'Refresh' text
Order row has '.refrigerated-row' class with blue gradient background
Frost badge (❄️) displayed next to order number
.badge-refrigerated CSS class provides white badge with blue background
Order RX-QNS00001 (ID 1) shows refrigerated styling
Edit modal contains checkbox with id='m-refrigerated'
Checkbox label shows '❄️ Refrigerated Item (Keep Cold)'
Checkbox state is preserved when modal opens
doUpdate() function sends isRefrigerated to API
GET /api/driver-portal/deliveries returns only: assigned, picked_up, out_for_delivery, delivering_now
in_transit orders are filtered out in controller query
Verified via API call - in_transit orders not in response
When driver sets status to in_transit via /api/driver-portal/deliveries/{id}/status
Order's DriverId and DriverName are set to null
Order remains in 'in_transit' status for admin to reassign
Tested: Order 3 had driverId=1, after in_transit became driverId=null
Order with status 'delivering_now' shows 'Complete Delivery (POD)' button
Order with status 'delivering_now' shows 'Failed Delivery' button
No scan required to access POD - buttons shown directly on card
POD modal has: recipient name, photo (required), signature (optional), notes
GET /api/driver-portal/history endpoint returns 200
Delivery History tab visible on driver portal
History includes: delivered, failed, cancelled orders
History includes POD fields: photoUrl, signatureUrl, recipientNameSigned
GET /api/admin/orders includes isRefrigerated in each order object
Order 1 shows isRefrigerated: true
Field is boolean type (true/false)

ui_flows_tested: Admin login and Orders page navigation
Refresh button on Admin Orders page
Refrigerated order styling (blue row, frost badge)
Active Deliveries and Delivery History tabs
rca_of_the_issue: Minor issue found: /api/orders/{id} endpoint was missing isRefrigerated field in the Select projection. Fixed by adding o.IsRefrigerated to the query.
